verses.text: David begs God not to rebuke him in wrath, for His arrows pierce him and His hand presses him down. He feels sin's chastening. The believer under discipline cries for mercy, not for justice.
David begs God not to rebuke him in wrath, for His arrows pierce him and His hand presses him down. He feels sin's chastening. The believer under discipline cries for mercy, not for justice.
David begs God not to rebuke him in wrath, for His arrows pierce him and His hand presses him down. He feels sin's chastening. The believer under discipline cries for mercy, not for justice.
His iniquities go over his head like a heavy burden, his wounds fester, and he is bowed down all the day. Sin is felt as a crushing weight. There is no rest while guilt lies unhealed upon the soul.
His iniquities go over his head like a heavy burden, his wounds fester, and he is bowed down all the day. Sin is felt as a crushing weight. There is no rest while guilt lies unhealed upon the soul.
His iniquities go over his head like a heavy burden, his wounds fester, and he is bowed down all the day. Sin is felt as a crushing weight. There is no rest while guilt lies unhealed upon the soul.
His body is full of disease and he groans from the disquiet of his heart. Soul and body suffer together. The whole man feels the misery of unforgiven sin.
His body is full of disease and he groans from the disquiet of his heart. Soul and body suffer together. The whole man feels the misery of unforgiven sin.
'Lord, all my desire is before thee,' he says; his heart pants and his strength fails. He lays his longing bare before God. The praying soul hides nothing from the One who already sees all.
'Lord, all my desire is before thee,' he says; his heart pants and his strength fails. He lays his longing bare before God. The praying soul hides nothing from the One who already sees all.
His friends keep their distance and his enemies lay snares against him. He is deserted and hunted at once. Affliction often strips away human comfort along with health.
His friends keep their distance and his enemies lay snares against him. He is deserted and hunted at once. Affliction often strips away human comfort along with health.
Yet he is like a deaf, mute man, not answering his slanderers. He commits his defence to God. Silence under reproach can be the strength of one who trusts the Judge.
Yet he is like a deaf, mute man, not answering his slanderers. He commits his defence to God. Silence under reproach can be the strength of one who trusts the Judge.
'For in thee, O LORD, do I hope: thou wilt hear,' lest his foes rejoice over him. Hope steadies him. The soul that waits on God can bear the taunts of men.
'For in thee, O LORD, do I hope: thou wilt hear,' lest his foes rejoice over him. Hope steadies him. The soul that waits on God can bear the taunts of men.

Ready to fall, he confesses his iniquity and grieves over his sin, while strong enemies hate him for doing good. He owns his guilt yet keeps his integrity toward men. Honest confession to God and a clear conscience toward others go together.

Ready to fall, he confesses his iniquity and grieves over his sin, while strong enemies hate him for doing good. He owns his guilt yet keeps his integrity toward men. Honest confession to God and a clear conscience toward others go together.

Ready to fall, he confesses his iniquity and grieves over his sin, while strong enemies hate him for doing good. He owns his guilt yet keeps his integrity toward men. Honest confession to God and a clear conscience toward others go together.

Ready to fall, he confesses his iniquity and grieves over his sin, while strong enemies hate him for doing good. He owns his guilt yet keeps his integrity toward men. Honest confession to God and a clear conscience toward others go together.
'Forsake me not, O LORD... make haste to help me, O Lord my salvation.' He ends clinging to God. When all else fails, the soul casts itself wholly on the God of its salvation.
'Forsake me not, O LORD... make haste to help me, O Lord my salvation.' He ends clinging to God. When all else fails, the soul casts itself wholly on the God of its salvation.
